title: Decide Bureau Ops packaging path
goal: Decide the future packaging shape for operational organs after the documentation and audit tasks are complete.

metadata.outcome: Implemented in Bureau PR #243: documented the Bureau Ops packaging decision. Bureau Core and Bureau Ops stay in the existing bureau Python distribution for now; bureau-ops extraction is blocked until grouped aliases, boundary checks, CI separation, deployment migration evidence and lower operator cost are proven.
validation: Bureau PR #243 GitHub CI passed: validate (3.10) and validate (3.12).
Local pre-merge: PYTHONPATH=src pytest tests/test_ops_packaging_decision.py -q -> 5 passed.
Local pre-merge: PYTHONPATH=src make validate -> 370 passed.
Local pre-merge: PYTHONPATH=src python3 -m bureau.cli --root . check -> valid True.
Local pre-merge: git diff --check -> pass after whitespace fix.
Local post-merge on origin/main: PYTHONPATH=src pytest tests/test_ops_packaging_decision.py -q -> 5 passed.
Local post-merge on origin/main: PYTHONPATH=src python3 -m bureau.cli --root . check -> valid True.

verified_acceptance.packaging-criteria: Decision doc states extraction criteria: stable Core API, entry-point stability, deployment portability, CI coverage, operator cost and reversibility; extraction is blocked until criteria are met.
verified_acceptance.api-contract: Decision doc identifies stable Core API surfaces for Ops consumers: registry schemas, queue JSON, bureau.cli check, guard reports, state-root doctor, rLens policy report and entry-point inventory; legacy.py and arbitrary StateStore internals remain unstable.
verified_acceptance.deployment-cost: Decision doc weighs systemd reference deployment, packaging, CI, operator cost, failure diagnosis and reversibility; six known service/timer pairs are called out as migration cost.

acceptance.assertion: Decision identifies the stable Bureau Core API surface.
Decision weighs systemd, packaging, CI and operator-cost implications.
